title: micranthes lyallii subsp. hultenii
intro: Micranthes lyallii subsp. hultenii, also known as Lyall's saxifrage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the saxifrage family. It is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is recognized by its white or pink flowers and distinctive foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may be beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Lyall's saxifrage often include infusions or tinctures made from the plant's leaves and flowers, and are believed to promote healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
